title: NIS-2 · Art. 21 Security Measures
subtitle: Network and Information Security Directive 2 · {{count}} measures

essential.title: Essential entities (Annex I)
essential.description: Energy, transport, banking, financial market infrastructure, health, drinking water, wastewater, digital infrastructure, ICT services, space, public administration.

important.title: Important entities (Annex II)
important.description: Postal and courier services, waste management, chemicals, food, manufacturing, digital services, research. All Art. 21 measures apply to both categories.

empty.title: No NIS-2 measures yet
empty.description: Load the integrated NIS-2 catalogue (Art. 21) to start tracking implementation.

filterEmpty: No measures match the filter criteria.

modal.evidencePlaceholder: Reference to documents, policies or measures

categories.riskPolicies: Análisis de Riesgos y Políticas de Seguridad
categories.supplychainSecurity: Seguridad de la Cadena de Suministro
categories.accessControl: Seguridad del Personal y Control de Acceso

Art. 21(2)(a).title: Policies on risk analysis and information system security
Art. 21(2)(a).description: Establishment and implementation of policies on risk analysis and information system security.

Art. 21(2)(b).title: Incident handling
Art. 21(2)(b).description: Policies and procedures for incident handling (prevention, detection and response).

Art. 21(2)(c).title: Business continuity and crisis management
Art. 21(2)(c).description: Measures for business continuity, such as backup management and disaster recovery, and crisis management.

Art. 21(2)(d).title: Supply chain security
Art. 21(2)(d).description: Supply chain security, including security-related aspects concerning the relationships between each entity and its direct suppliers or service providers.

Art. 21(2)(e).title: Security in acquisition, development and maintenance
Art. 21(2)(e).description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

Art. 21(2)(f).title: Assessing the effectiveness of risk-management measures
Art. 21(2)(f).description: Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures.

Art. 21(2)(g).title: Cyber hygiene and training
Art. 21(2)(g).description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training.

Art. 21(2)(h).title: Use of cryptography and encryption
Art. 21(2)(h).description: Políticas y procedimientos relativos al uso de la criptografía y, en su caso, del cifrado.

Art. 21(2)(i).title: Human resources security, access control and asset management
Art. 21(2)(i).description: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management.

Art. 21(2)(j).title: Multi-factor authentication and secured communications
Art. 21(2)(j).description: Use of multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions, secured voice, video and text communications and secured emergency communication systems within the entity, where appropriate.

Art. 23(1).title: Early warning for significant incidents (within 24h)
Art. 23(1).description: Submission of an early warning without undue delay, and in any event within 24 hours of becoming aware of the significant incident.

Art. 23(2).title: Incident notification (within 72h)
Art. 23(2).description: Submission of an incident notification without undue delay, and in any event within 72 hours of becoming aware of the significant incident.

Art. 23(4).title: Final report (no later than one month after notification)
Art. 23(4).description: Submission of a final report no later than one month after the submission of the incident notification.
